Name: ValueStream
Notes: An end-to-end collection of activities that create a result for a customer, who may be the ultimate customer or an internal end-user of the value stream.

Name: ValueStage
Notes: A distinct, identifiable phase or step within a value stream that has a unique name, entrance criteria, exit criteria, and identifiable participating stakeholder(s)

Name: ValueItem
Notes: The judgment of worth, made by an individual or organization, attached to something tangible or intangible and attained in the course of a particular interaction with one or more other parties.The ValueItem represents the value of an Outcome produced by a Capability that supports a ValueStage as seen by a Stakeholder in the context of a ValueStream that contains the ValueStage. In general, this could have a yes/no value, a number value that represents some positive or negative value judgement, or a multi-dimensional evaluation.In the absence of a stakeholder, the value item could be used to represent a "transfer price" that is an artificial value associated with one or more outcomes that are intermediaries in a value streamThe rationale for a value represented by a value item is expressed by the value proposition meta-model element.

Name: Text
Notes: Constraint: each ValueStage aggregated in a ValueStream must be connected to one and only one different ValueStage in the same ValueStream by a successorStage association class that is composed by the ValueStream.Furthermore, one ValueStage is designated as the starting stage and it may not have a predecessor ValueStage. one ValueStage is designated as the ending stage and it may not have a successor ValueStage. The starting and ending stages may be the same stage.

Name: Stakeholder
Notes: An internal or external individual or organization with a vested interest in achieving value through a particular outcome.

Name: Capability
Notes: A particular ability or capacity that a business may possess or exchange to achieve a specific purpose or outcome.

Name: Information
Notes: Data that has been verified to be accurate and timely, is specific and organized for a purpose, is presented within a context that gives it meaning and relevance, and that can lead to an increase in understanding and decrease in uncertainty.

Name: CapabilityInstance
Notes: A specific realization of a capability, as it exists or is envisioned to exist, in the context of a given business unit, value stream stage, or other situational context.

Name: BusinessUnit
Notes: A logical element or segment of a company (such as accounting, production, marketing) representing a specific business function, and a definite place on the organizational chart, under the domain of a manager. Also called department, division, or functional area.

Name: BusinessModel
Notes: A business model describes the rationale of how an organization creates, delivers, and captures value.

Name: Objective
Notes: A quantitative, measurable result that defines strategy.An Objective is a description of a state of affairs that should result from execution of the strategy.

Name: CourseOfAction
Notes: A CourseOfAction is a description of a group of actions that should result in the achievement of the Objective. A particular Objective may possibly be achieved by many different CoursesOfAction.

Name: Rationale
Notes: A Rationale is an explanation of the reasoning leading to the conclusion that the CourseOfAction should result in the achievement of the Objective.A Rationale also explains why an Outcome is needed in order to be able to execute the CourseOfAction.

A Strategy is a composition of a CourseOfAction and its decompositions, the associated ActionPlans, the Objectives, the Rationales connecting the CoursesOfAction and the Objectives, and the Associations that connect these element to form a complete Strategy, There may be multiple Strategies, and they may share any of these elements.

Name: Initiative
Notes: A course of action that is being executed or has been selected for execution.

Name: ValueProposition
Notes: An innovation, service, or feature intended to make a company, product, or service attractive to customers or related stakeholders. The value proposition in the business architecture meta-model is a hypothetical description of how and why a stakeholder would see a net benefit or net detriment to the overall experience of acquiring the innovation, product or service The value proposition is associated with a stakeholder by way of the value item.

Name: ValuePropositionType
Notes: The value proposition type is a template that can be customized to create a particular value proposition.
